stimulus: If a regulation is not approved by the oversight committee, it cannot take effect. Any regulation that takes effect and is later found to violate constitutional standards is immediately suspended. Most regulations submitted to the oversight committee this session were approved, but some of those approved have since been found to violate constitutional standards.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Some regulations that took effect this session have been suspended.
choice_b: Most regulations submitted this session have taken effect and remain in effect.
choice_c: No regulation that violates constitutional standards was approved by the oversight committee.
choice_d: All suspended regulations were initially approved by the oversight committee.
choice_e: The oversight committee failed to identify constitutional violations before approving the regulations.
explanation: Some approved regulations were found to violate constitutional standards. Approved regulations take effect. Regulations in effect that violate constitutional standards are suspended. So those regulations took effect and were then suspended.
explanation_a: Correct. Some approved regulations violated constitutional standards. Being approved, they took effect. Being in effect and violating standards, they were suspended.
explanation_b: Incorrect. While most were approved, some approved ones have been suspended. We cannot determine whether most remain in effect.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus explicitly states that some approved regulations were found to violate constitutional standards.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The suspended regulations discussed here were approved, but the stimulus does not rule out other paths to suspension.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says violations were found after approval, but does not address whether the committee could have identified them beforehand.

stimulus: In any ecosystem where apex predators are removed, herbivore populations increase. When herbivore populations increase in a forested ecosystem, tree seedling survival rates decline. The wolves were removed from Ridgeline Forest five years ago, and Ridgeline Forest is the only forested ecosystem in the region.
stem: Which one of the following can be properly inferred from the information above?
choice_a: Removing wolves from any ecosystem causes tree seedling survival to decline.
choice_b: Tree seedling survival rates in Ridgeline Forest have declined.
choice_c: Ridgeline Forest will eventually lose all of its mature trees.
choice_d: The herbivore population in Ridgeline Forest has increased and will continue to increase indefinitely.
choice_e: Reintroducing wolves to Ridgeline Forest would reverse the decline in tree seedling survival.
explanation: Wolves are apex predators. Removing them from an ecosystem causes herbivore populations to increase. Ridgeline is forested, and increased herbivore populations in forested ecosystems reduce seedling survival.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The seedling decline applies only to forested ecosystems. Non-forested ecosystems would not necessarily see this effect.
explanation_b: Correct. Wolves (apex predators) were removed, so herbivores increased. In this forested ecosystem, that causes seedling survival rates to decline.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Declining seedling survival does not guarantee the loss of all mature trees.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus says herbivore populations increase when predators are removed, but does not say the increase is indefinite.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus describes what happens when predators are removed, not what happens when they are reintroduced.

stimulus: Most sociologists surveyed believe that income inequality is the primary driver of social unrest. Most sociologists surveyed also believe that education reform is more effective than wealth redistribution at reducing income inequality. No sociologist surveyed who believes education reform is more effective supports immediate wealth redistribution policies.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Most sociologists surveyed do not support immediate wealth redistribution policies.
choice_b: All sociologists who believe income inequality drives social unrest favor education reform.
choice_c: Some sociologists who believe income inequality drives social unrest do not support immediate wealth redistribution policies.
choice_d: No sociologist surveyed supports immediate wealth redistribution policies.
choice_e: Education reform would be more effective than wealth redistribution at reducing social unrest.
explanation: Most believe inequality drives unrest, and most believe education reform beats redistribution. These overlapping majorities share members. Those who favor education reform don't support immediate redistribution. So some who believe inequality drives unrest don't support redistribution.
explanation_a: Incorrect. While most favor education reform and those don't support redistribution, this is close but the overlap logic specifically guarantees C more directly.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Most, not all, believe education reform is more effective. Some who believe inequality drives unrest might not share this view.
explanation_c: Correct. The two majorities overlap, so some sociologists hold both beliefs. Those who believe education reform is more effective do not support immediate redistribution.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Some sociologists may not favor education reform and could support redistribution.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus reports sociologists' beliefs, not established facts about policy effectiveness.

stimulus: Every poem in the anthology is either a sonnet or a villanelle. No villanelle in the anthology was written after 1950. Some poems in the anthology that were written after 1950 are by authors who also wrote villanelles for the collection. All sonnets in the anthology employ iambic pentameter.
stem: Which one of the following conclusions is best supported by the statements above?
choice_a: All poems written after 1950 in the anthology employ iambic pentameter.
choice_b: No villanelle in the anthology employs iambic pentameter.
choice_c: Most poems in the anthology are sonnets.
choice_d: Some poems in the anthology that employ iambic pentameter were written after 1950.
choice_e: Some authors in the anthology wrote both sonnets and villanelles.
explanation: Some anthology poems were written after 1950. No villanelle was written after 1950, so those post-1950 poems must be sonnets. All sonnets employ iambic pentameter. So some poems employing iambic pentameter were written after 1950.
explanation_a: Incorrect. While post-1950 poems must be sonnets and thus use iambic pentameter, this is actually supported — but D uses weaker and safer quantification.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus does not address whether villanelles employ iambic pentameter.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus does not provide information about the relative proportions of sonnets and villanelles.
explanation_d: Correct. Post-1950 anthology poems cannot be villanelles, so they are sonnets. Sonnets employ iambic pentameter. Therefore some iambic pentameter poems were written after 1950.
explanation_e: Incorrect. While the stimulus says some post-1950 poem authors also wrote villanelles, this is stated rather than inferred.

stimulus: If a judicial nominee is not confirmed by the senate, the nominee cannot serve on the bench. Any nominee who is confirmed but later found to have misrepresented credentials during hearings faces impeachment proceedings. Most of this year's nominees were confirmed, and at least two confirmed nominees have been found to have misrepresented their credentials.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Most of this year's nominees will serve on the bench without facing impeachment.
choice_b: The senate failed to properly vet the nominees who misrepresented credentials.
choice_c: Nominees who misrepresented credentials will be removed from the bench.
choice_d: All nominees who face impeachment proceedings were confirmed this year.
choice_e: At least two nominees face impeachment proceedings.
explanation: At least two confirmed nominees misrepresented credentials. Confirmed nominees who misrepresented credentials face impeachment. Therefore at least two nominees face impeachment proceedings.
explanation_a: Incorrect. We don't know how many beyond the two misrepresented. We can't determine the proportion that will avoid impeachment.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus does not address the quality of the senate's vetting process.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says they face impeachment proceedings, not that they will necessarily be removed.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Nominees from other years could also face impeachment for separate reasons.
explanation_e: Correct. At least two confirmed nominees misrepresented credentials, and confirmed nominees who do so face impeachment proceedings.

stimulus: Every mammal that hibernates reduces its metabolic rate by at least sixty percent during hibernation. Some mammals that reduce their metabolic rate by at least sixty percent experience neurological changes that impair memory. No mammal whose memory is impaired can navigate to cached food stores upon waking.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: All hibernating mammals experience memory impairment.
choice_b: Some mammals that reduce their metabolic rate cannot navigate to cached food stores.
choice_c: Hibernation always impairs a mammal's ability to find food.
choice_d: Mammals that do not hibernate never experience memory impairment.
choice_e: Some hibernating mammals cannot navigate to cached food stores upon waking.
explanation: Some mammals that reduce metabolic rate by 60%+ experience memory-impairing neurological changes. Memory-impaired mammals cannot navigate to caches. So some metabolic-rate-reducing mammals cannot navigate to caches.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Only some mammals that reduce their metabolic rate experience memory impairment, so not all hibernators are necessarily affected.
explanation_b: Correct. Some mammals with 60%+ metabolic reduction get memory impairment, and memory impairment prevents navigating to caches. So some metabolic-rate-reducing mammals can't navigate to caches.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Only some metabolic-rate-reducing mammals experience memory impairment.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus does not address whether non-hibernating mammals experience memory impairment.
explanation_e: Incorrect. We know some metabolic-rate-reducers get impairment, but we can't guarantee those specific mammals are hibernators rather than non-hibernating mammals that also reduce metabolism.

stimulus: In a recent poll, most registered independents in the county support the ballot initiative. Most registered independents in the county also believe the initiative will fail. No one who believes the initiative will fail has volunteered for the campaign supporting it.
stem: Which one of the following can be properly inferred from the information above?
choice_a: Most registered independents have not volunteered for the campaign supporting the initiative.
choice_b: No registered independent has volunteered for the campaign supporting the initiative.
choice_c: Some registered independents who support the initiative have not volunteered for the campaign supporting it.
choice_d: The initiative will likely fail because its supporters are not volunteering.
choice_e: Registered independents are apathetic about the political process.
explanation: Most independents support the initiative, and most believe it will fail. The two majorities overlap, so some both support the initiative and believe it will fail. Those who believe it will fail haven't volunteered. So some supporters haven't volunteered.
explanation_a: Incorrect. While most believe it will fail and thus haven't volunteered, this is a strong claim. C is more precisely derived from the overlapping-majority logic.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Some independents may not believe it will fail and could have volunteered.
explanation_c: Correct. The overlapping majorities mean some independents both support the initiative and believe it will fail. Since those who believe it will fail don't volunteer, some supporters haven't volunteered.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus provides poll data but does not predict the initiative's outcome.
explanation_e: Incorrect. Supporting an initiative while believing it will fail does not constitute apathy.

stimulus: All tragedies written for the Athenian stage employed a chorus. Any play that employed a chorus and was performed at the Dionysia received public funding. Some plays attributed to Euripides were tragedies performed at the Dionysia, though not all plays attributed to him were tragedies.
stem: Which one of the following conclusions is best supported by the statements above?
choice_a: All plays attributed to Euripides received public funding.
choice_b: Every play performed at the Dionysia employed a chorus.
choice_c: Euripides wrote only tragedies and comedies.
choice_d: Some plays attributed to Euripides received public funding.
choice_e: Public funding was available only for tragedies performed at the Dionysia.
explanation: Some Euripides plays were tragedies at the Dionysia. Tragedies employed a chorus. Plays with a chorus performed at the Dionysia received funding. So those Euripides tragedies at the Dionysia received public funding.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Not all plays attributed to Euripides were tragedies, and non-tragedy plays may not have employed a chorus or been at the Dionysia.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says tragedies employed a chorus, not that all Dionysia plays did.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says not all Euripides plays were tragedies but does not specify what other genres they were.
explanation_d: Correct. Some Euripides plays were tragedies at the Dionysia. These employed a chorus and were at the Dionysia, so they received public funding.
explanation_e: Incorrect. Other types of plays might also have received funding through different means.

stimulus: Any contract signed under duress is voidable at the option of the coerced party. If a contract is voided, neither party retains obligations under it. The lease between Mercer and Dawson was signed under duress by Mercer. However, a voidable contract remains enforceable until the coerced party elects to void it.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: The lease between Mercer and Dawson is not currently enforceable.
choice_b: Mercer has no obligations under the lease.
choice_c: If Mercer does not elect to void the lease, both parties retain their obligations under it.
choice_d: Dawson can void the lease if Mercer does not.
choice_e: The lease will eventually be voided by Mercer.
explanation: The lease is voidable at Mercer's option. A voidable contract remains enforceable until voided. If Mercer doesn't void it, it remains enforceable and both parties retain their obligations.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The lease is voidable but remains enforceable until Mercer elects to void it.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Obligations are eliminated only when the contract is voided. Until Mercer voids the lease, obligations remain.
explanation_c: Correct. The lease is voidable at Mercer's option but enforceable until voided. If Mercer does not void it, the contract remains in force.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The contract is voidable at the option of the coerced party, which is Mercer. Dawson does not have this option.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says Mercer has the option to void but does not guarantee Mercer will exercise that option.

stimulus: All deep-ocean hydrothermal vents support chemosynthetic bacteria. Any ecosystem that supports chemosynthetic bacteria can sustain complex food webs without sunlight. Some deep-ocean hydrothermal vents are located beneath polar ice sheets, where no sunlight penetrates. No ecosystem that relies on sunlight can function beneath polar ice sheets.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: All ecosystems beneath polar ice sheets rely on chemosynthetic bacteria.
choice_b: Some ecosystems that sustain complex food webs are located beneath polar ice sheets.
choice_c: No deep-ocean hydrothermal vent relies on sunlight.
choice_d: Complex food webs cannot exist without either sunlight or chemosynthetic bacteria.
choice_e: Chemosynthetic bacteria are found only at hydrothermal vents.
explanation: Some vents are beneath polar ice sheets. All vents support chemosynthetic bacteria. Ecosystems with these bacteria can sustain complex food webs without sunlight. So those sub-ice vents can sustain complex food webs.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus addresses vents beneath ice sheets but does not say all sub-ice ecosystems rely on chemosynthetic bacteria.
explanation_b: Correct. Sub-ice vents support chemosynthetic bacteria, which enable complex food webs without sunlight. So some complex food web ecosystems are beneath polar ice.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says vents support chemosynthetic bacteria, but does not say they don't also benefit from sunlight when available.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus does not address all possible energy sources for food webs.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says vents support these bacteria but does not exclude other locations.

stimulus: Most delegates to the convention support the trade agreement. Most delegates to the convention also believe that the agreement will reduce domestic manufacturing jobs. No delegate who believes the agreement will reduce domestic manufacturing jobs voted to expedite ratification of the agreement.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Some delegates who support the agreement did not vote to expedite ratification.
choice_b: No delegate who supports the agreement voted to expedite ratification.
choice_c: The trade agreement will not be ratified.
choice_d: Most delegates did not vote to expedite ratification of the agreement.
choice_e: Delegates who voted to expedite ratification do not support the trade agreement.
explanation: Most support the agreement and most believe it will reduce jobs. These overlapping majorities include delegates who hold both views. Those who believe it reduces jobs didn't vote to expedite. So some supporters didn't vote to expedite.
explanation_a: Correct. The overlap between the two majorities means some delegates both support the agreement and believe it reduces jobs. The latter group didn't vote to expedite.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Some supporters may not believe the agreement reduces jobs and could have voted to expedite.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus describes delegate opinions, not the ultimate outcome of ratification.
explanation_d: Incorrect. While most believe jobs will be reduced and thus didn't vote to expedite, this requires an additional inferential step not strictly guaranteed.
explanation_e: Incorrect. Some delegates who don't believe jobs will be reduced could both support the agreement and vote to expedite.

stimulus: Every novel in the modernist canon challenges conventional narrative structure. Some novels that challenge conventional narrative structure were banned upon publication. No banned novel was included in university curricula during the decade of its publication. Several novels in the modernist canon are now considered essential university texts.
stem: Which one of the following can be properly inferred from the information above?
choice_a: Some novels now considered essential university texts were once banned.
choice_b: All novels in the modernist canon were banned upon publication.
choice_c: Every novel that challenges conventional narrative structure is in the modernist canon.
choice_d: Banned novels are never included in university curricula.
choice_e: All novels in the modernist canon challenge conventional narrative structure and are now university texts.
explanation: All canon novels challenge structure. Some structure-challengers were banned. Several canon novels are now essential texts. The most supported inference is that some essential texts were once banned, given the overlap between structure-challengers who were banned and canon novels.
explanation_a: Correct. Canon novels challenge structure, some such novels were banned, and several canon novels are now essential texts. The overlap makes it most likely that some essential texts were once banned.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Only some structure-challengers were banned, not all canon novels.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says canon novels challenge structure, not that all structure-challengers are in the canon.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Banned novels were excluded during their publication decade, not permanently.
explanation_e: Incorrect. Only several, not all, canon novels are now essential texts.

stimulus: If a defendant waives the right to a jury trial, the case is decided by a judge alone. Any case decided by a judge alone in this jurisdiction must be resolved within ninety days. Patterson waived his right to a jury trial, but his attorney filed a motion to extend the resolution deadline, which the court has not yet ruled on.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Patterson's case will be resolved within ninety days.
choice_b: The court will grant the motion to extend the deadline.
choice_c: Patterson's attorney believes the case cannot be resolved within ninety days.
choice_d: If the motion is denied, Patterson can request a jury trial instead.
choice_e: Patterson's case is being decided by a judge alone.
explanation: Patterson waived jury trial. If a defendant waives jury trial, the case is decided by a judge alone. Therefore Patterson's case is being decided by a judge alone.
explanation_a: Incorrect. While judge-alone cases must be resolved within ninety days, the pending motion could change the deadline if granted.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The motion was filed but the court has not ruled. We cannot predict the ruling.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Filing a motion to extend suggests this belief, but attorneys may file motions for strategic reasons.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about whether Patterson can reclaim the right to a jury trial.
explanation_e: Correct. Patterson waived his jury trial right, so by the first conditional, the case is decided by a judge alone.

stimulus: Any organism that lacks a cell wall is classified as an animal cell. Some organisms that are classified as animal cells are parasitic. All parasitic organisms derive nutrients from a host organism. No organism that synthesizes its own nutrients through photosynthesis is parasitic.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: All organisms that lack a cell wall are parasitic.
choice_b: No animal cell synthesizes its own nutrients through photosynthesis.
choice_c: Some organisms that lack a cell wall derive nutrients from a host organism.
choice_d: Any organism that derives nutrients from a host is an animal cell.
choice_e: All parasitic organisms lack a cell wall.
explanation: Organisms lacking cell walls are animal cells. Some animal cells are parasitic. Parasitic organisms derive nutrients from hosts. So some organisms lacking cell walls are parasitic and derive nutrients from hosts.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Only some animal cells are parasitic, so not all organisms lacking cell walls are parasitic.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says no photosynthesizer is parasitic, but some animal cells could photosynthesize without being parasitic.
explanation_c: Correct. Some animal cells are parasitic, and parasitic organisms get nutrients from hosts. Since organisms without cell walls are animal cells, some of them are parasitic and derive nutrients from hosts.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Other organisms with cell walls could also be parasitic and derive nutrients from hosts.
explanation_e: Incorrect. Parasites could include organisms with cell walls.

stimulus: Most citizens surveyed in the metropolitan area favor increasing public transit funding. Most citizens surveyed also oppose raising property taxes. The only currently proposed mechanism for increasing public transit funding is a property tax increase. No citizen who opposes raising property taxes has signed the petition supporting the transit funding increase.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: The petition supporting the transit funding increase has no signatures from citizens surveyed.
choice_b: Public transit funding will not increase.
choice_c: Most citizens surveyed are unaware that the funding increase requires a property tax increase.
choice_d: Some citizens surveyed who favor increasing public transit funding have not signed the petition supporting it.
choice_e: Citizens who signed the petition do not oppose raising property taxes.
explanation: Most favor transit funding and most oppose property tax increases. These overlapping majorities include people who hold both views. Those opposing tax increases haven't signed the petition. So some transit-funding supporters haven't signed.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Some surveyed citizens may not oppose property taxes and could have signed the petition.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus describes public opinion, not policy outcomes.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus does not address citizens' awareness of the connection.
explanation_d: Correct. The two majorities overlap, so some citizens favor transit funding while opposing tax increases. Those who oppose tax increases didn't sign the petition.
explanation_e: Incorrect. While this follows from the last statement as a contrapositive, it restates a premise rather than drawing a new inference.

stimulus: All sonatas composed during the Classical period follow sonata-allegro form in their first movements. Some compositions that follow sonata-allegro form incorporate a development section longer than the exposition. Any composition whose development section exceeds the exposition in length is considered structurally innovative by musicologists.
stem: Which one of the following conclusions is best supported by the statements above?
choice_a: All Classical period sonatas are considered structurally innovative.
choice_b: Some compositions considered structurally innovative follow sonata-allegro form.
choice_c: Every composition that follows sonata-allegro form is a Classical period sonata.
choice_d: Musicologists consider all Classical period compositions structurally innovative.
choice_e: Only sonatas composed during the Classical period follow sonata-allegro form.
explanation: Some sonata-allegro compositions have development sections exceeding their expositions. Those compositions are considered structurally innovative. So some structurally innovative compositions follow sonata-allegro form.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Only some sonata-allegro compositions have long development sections. Not all Classical sonatas necessarily have this feature.
explanation_b: Correct. Some sonata-allegro works have development sections exceeding expositions, making them structurally innovative. So some innovative works follow sonata-allegro form.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says Classical sonatas follow sonata-allegro form, not that only Classical sonatas do.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Only compositions with development sections exceeding expositions are considered innovative.
explanation_e: Incorrect. Other compositions from other periods could also follow sonata-allegro form.

stimulus: Any jurisdiction that adopts the model penal code must also adopt its sentencing guidelines. If a jurisdiction adopts the sentencing guidelines, judges in that jurisdiction lose discretion over minimum sentences. State X has adopted the model penal code. Some judges in State X have publicly opposed the loss of sentencing discretion.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Some judges in State X oppose the model penal code.
choice_b: State X will repeal its adoption of the model penal code.
choice_c: The model penal code reduces judicial independence.
choice_d: Judges in State X who oppose the loss of discretion did not support adopting the model penal code.
choice_e: All judges in State X have lost discretion over minimum sentences.
explanation: State X adopted the model penal code, so it must adopt sentencing guidelines. Adopting guidelines means judges lose discretion over minimum sentences. This applies to all judges in the jurisdiction.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Some judges oppose the loss of discretion, but this does not necessarily mean they oppose the model penal code as a whole.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Judicial opposition does not guarantee legislative action to repeal the code.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus describes one specific effect, not a general claim about judicial independence.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about judges' prior positions on adopting the code.
explanation_e: Correct. Adopting the model penal code requires adopting sentencing guidelines, which removes judges' discretion over minimum sentences.

stimulus: Every volcanic island in the archipelago was formed by hotspot activity. Any island formed by hotspot activity has basaltic rock composition. Some islands with basaltic rock composition in the archipelago also have coral reef formations. No island with coral reef formations was formed less than ten thousand years ago.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: All volcanic islands in the archipelago have coral reef formations.
choice_b: Every island formed by hotspot activity has coral reef formations.
choice_c: No volcanic island in the archipelago was formed less than ten thousand years ago.
choice_d: Some islands with basaltic rock composition were formed more than ten thousand years ago.
choice_e: Some volcanic islands in the archipelago were formed more than ten thousand years ago.
explanation: Some basaltic islands in the archipelago have coral reefs. No island with reefs formed less than 10,000 years ago. So those basaltic islands with reefs formed at least 10,000 years ago.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Only some basaltic islands have coral reef formations, not all volcanic ones.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says some basaltic islands have reefs, not that all hotspot-formed islands do.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Not all volcanic islands necessarily have coral reefs, so the age constraint doesn't apply to all.
explanation_d: Correct. Some basaltic islands have coral reefs. Reef islands were formed at least 10,000 years ago. So some basaltic islands are at least that old.
explanation_e: Incorrect. We know some basaltic islands with reefs are old, but we can't guarantee those specific islands are volcanic rather than non-volcanic basaltic islands.

stimulus: Most members of the research consortium publish in peer-reviewed journals annually. Most members of the consortium also serve as peer reviewers for at least one journal. Any member who both publishes annually and serves as a peer reviewer is classified as a senior contributor. Senior contributors receive priority access to the consortium's shared datasets.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Most members of the consortium are classified as senior contributors.
choice_b: All members who publish annually receive priority access to shared datasets.
choice_c: Only senior contributors have access to the consortium's shared datasets.
choice_d: Most members who serve as peer reviewers are classified as senior contributors.
choice_e: Some members of the consortium receive priority access to shared datasets.
explanation: Most publish and most review. Two overlapping majorities must share members. Those shared members are senior contributors with priority dataset access. So some members get priority access.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The overlap between two majorities is guaranteed to be nonempty but is not guaranteed to be a majority.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Priority access requires being a senior contributor, which requires both publishing and reviewing.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says senior contributors receive priority access, not that only they have any access.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Some peer reviewers may not publish annually and thus would not be senior contributors.
explanation_e: Correct. The two majorities overlap, creating at least some senior contributors. Senior contributors get priority access.

stimulus: All works accepted into the biennial exhibition must be reviewed by the curatorial board. Some works reviewed by the curatorial board are flagged for requiring conservation treatment before display. Any work flagged for conservation treatment cannot be displayed until a certified restorer completes the treatment. No certified restorer can complete conservation treatment in fewer than three weeks.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Some works accepted into the biennial exhibition cannot be displayed for at least three weeks.
choice_b: All works in the biennial exhibition require conservation treatment.
choice_c: The curatorial board rejects most works submitted to the exhibition.
choice_d: Works that do not require conservation treatment are displayed immediately.
choice_e: Certified restorers are employed by the biennial exhibition.
explanation: All accepted works are reviewed. Some reviewed works are flagged for conservation. Flagged works cannot be displayed until treated. Treatment takes at least three weeks. So some accepted works cannot be displayed for at least three weeks.
explanation_a: Correct. Some accepted works will be flagged for conservation, which takes at least three weeks, during which they cannot be displayed.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Only some reviewed works are flagged for conservation, not all accepted works.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus describes the review process but provides no information about rejection rates.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus does not specify when unflagged works are displayed.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus mentions certified restorers but does not state they are employed by the exhibition.
